summary: Matched trophy module work in the ty subsystem: the PR body says it achieved 100% matches for 14 functions and improved another 15 across toy.c, tylist.c, and tyfigupon.c. The diff adds substantial C implementations for trophy unlock/state handling, trophy data lookup, list/archive setup, fog/light callbacks, dev-text display helpers, and related ty headers/types. It also updates ifprize.c to call the new un_803063D4 signature with three arguments, and moves Kirby static data declaration for ftKb_Init_804D9570 into ftKb_Init.static.h. Review focused on preserving existing TyModeState typing, avoiding unnecessary casts, fixing pointer math, extracting/justifying structs, and using inlined string accesses.
what_changed: Implemented many previously stubbed trophy/toy functions in src/melee/ty/toy.c, including visible diff examples un_803053C4, Trophy_SetUnlockState, un_803060BC, un_803062BC, un_80306930, un_80306A0C, un_80306C5C, un_80307F64, un_803082F8, un_80308328, un_80308354, un_803109A0, un_803114E8, and un_80311788.
Implemented large portions of src/melee/ty/tylist.c, including archive symbol loading and trophy list/table processing around un_803124BC and un_8031263C.

Added or expanded ty types in src/melee/ty/types.h, including TyModeState-related usage, ToyAnimState, ToyEntry, TrophyData, and additional Toy fields such as x19C, trophyTable, and trophyCount as shown in review excerpts.
Added local helper structs in toy.c such as TyViewData, TyDisplayData, and TyLightData to replace raw M2C_FIELD-style access in at least some places.
Updated ifprize.c caller un_802FE918 from un_803063D4() to un_803063D4(0, 0, 0), matching the new trophy function signature.
Moved ftKb_Init_804D9570 declaration out of ftKb_Init.c into ftKb_Init.static.h and kept ftKb_SpecialNMs_8010BC90 formatting/matching intact.
Adjusted ty headers toy.h, tylist.h, tyfigupon.h, and tydisplay.h to expose updated signatures/types needed by the matched functions.
The PR had merge conflicts before merge; maintainer requested they be resolved, and the author agreed to handle them.
smart_moves: Converted raw global memory accesses into named structs where evidence supported it, e.g. TyViewData for un_804D6E6C->x4 and ToyAnimState for state at un_804A26B8 + 0x3F0.

Kept trophy table logic close to original control flow, including pointer-walk loops, explicit byte offsets, gotos, and high-byte/low-byte bit manipulation, which is often necessary for matching old compiler output.
Updated call sites immediately when a matched function signature changed, preventing ifprize.c from retaining an obsolete zero-argument un_803063D4 call.
Recognized language-dependent trophy data behavior in un_803060BC by checking saved/current JP/US language settings and selecting JP or US table fields accordingly.
Used small field-specific data structs and header declarations instead of scattering anonymous externs and M2C_FIELD accesses everywhere, making later ty work more searchable.
Review pushed toward stronger struct extraction and cleaner pointer math rather than settling for cast-heavy M2C output.
decomp_lessons: When adding struct fields to a shared type like Toy, include clear justification from offsets, table size, caller behavior, or matching constraints; reviewers explicitly questioned confidence for fields like trophyTable and trophyCount.
Avoid unnecessary casts when function prototypes can be corrected instead. Review questioned casts around gmMainLib_8015CC78 and gmMainLib_8015CC84 returns.
Raw pointer math should be cleaned up file-wide once enough structure is known; review explicitly asked to fix all pointer math in tylist.c.
Do not regress known typed access back to M2C_FIELD. Review noted that tydisplay.c should not have reverted TyModeState usage.

For archive symbol names and string-table offsets, prefer the expected inlined string access pattern when matching requires it; reviewer asked to fix string accesses in tylist.c to use inlined strings.

If a decompiled expression points into a known global runtime object, try extracting a local struct instead of repeatedly using M2C_FIELD; review asked whether a struct was known or could be extracted.
Signature changes in decomp modules can cascade into unrelated callers; update all call sites in the same PR when practical.
For matching stack-copy/table-search code, preserving unusual pointer pre-decrement/pre-increment idioms and unrolled checks may be more effective than writing idiomatic loops.
naming_conventions: Module prefix remains ty/toy-style with unresolved symbols kept as un_8030xxxx and globals as un_804Dxxxx/un_804Axxxx when no semantic name is established.
Semantic names were introduced only where evidence was reasonably local: TyViewData, TyDisplayData, TyLightData, ToyAnimState, ToyEntry, TrophyData, Trophy_SetUnlockState.
Struct fields largely retain offset-style names such as x0E, x0F, x10, x11, x19A, x19C when semantics are not fully known.
Header/static declarations follow project convention with address comments, e.g. /* 4D9570 */ extern f32 ftKb_Init_804D9570; in ftKb_Init.static.h.
Function prototypes preserve existing unresolved names such as un_803060BC and un_803063D4 until broader naming confidence exists.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used gotos and labels such as check_found/check_found2/done to preserve original branch structure in trophy selection logic.
Used byte-offset pointer arithmetic against u16 trophy state tables to match low-byte unlock counts and high-bit flags, including xor with 0x8000.
Used explicit language-table sentinel scans with id == -1 in TrophyData arrays for un_803060BC.
Used stack table copying from un_803B8910 into ToyEntry table[9], including pointer pre-offsets and a do/while copy loop, to match un_803109A0.
Kept an artificial pad array in un_803114E8, documented as forcing a larger stack frame, indicating stack-frame shaping for match.
Used casts through byte pointers in some trophy table accesses; review feedback suggests these were matching-oriented but should be improved when better struct/table types are available.
Used inlined string-table offsets from un_803FDD18 for archive symbol loading and animation names; review indicated these string accesses needed the correct inline form.
Preserved low-level HSD object callback patterns for fog, lights, GObj procs, JObj animation removal/application, and DevText setup.

review_feedback.feedback: Do not revert typed TyModeState access back to M2C_FIELD-style access.
Questioned whether casts were necessary around table getter return values.
Requested fixing all pointer math in the file.
Asked whether an unknown structure could be identified and extracted instead of raw field access.
Asked for confidence and justification for newly introduced structs such as ToyAnimState/ToyEntry/TrophyData.
Asked for justification for added Toy fields including trophyTable and trophyCount.
Maintainer requested string accesses be fixed to use inlined strings.
Maintainer requested merge conflicts be resolved before merging.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or further justify ToyAnimState, ToyEntry, TrophyData, trophyTable, or trophyCount.
Search for subsequent tylist.c cleanup around pointer math and inlined string-table access from un_803FDD18.
Check later uses of un_803063D4 to confirm its three-argument signature and whether it received a semantic name.
Search for TyModeState regressions or review comments about avoiding M2C_FIELD in tydisplay.c.
Look for later PRs matching remaining toy.c stubs around un_80305058, un_80305918, un_803062EC, un_803063D4, un_80306954, un_80306A48, un_8030813C, un_803083D8, and un_803084A0.
